title: Ash and Silence
prompt: Extreme wide shot, a vast gray wasteland of petrified trees and cracked earth stretching under a bruised purple sky, a lone figure in dark traveling clothes walking along a broken road, Mira with dark hair pulled back and a cracked device glowing faintly on her forearm, distant ruins on the horizon, muted desaturated color palette, cold directional lighting from a dim sun, cinematic dark fantasy atmosphere.
narrative: Mira walks the Ashfield alone. Three weeks since the last settlement burned. The device on her arm hums faintly — a pre-collapse AI bracer, cracked but functional. It pulses when she passes buried infrastructure. She follows the signal.
narration: The Ashfield stretches in every direction. Gray dust, gray sky, gray silence. Mira has not spoken aloud in six days. The device on her forearm vibrates — a low, persistent hum against cracked skin. She does not stop. She does not look back. The last settlement burned because she arrived too late. The bodies still smelled of char when she buried them. Three weeks of walking since. Her boots are worn through. The device hums louder. There is something ahead. Something beneath the ash. She follows the signal because there is nothing else to follow.

title: The Broken Gate
prompt: Medium shot, Mira standing at a collapsed stone gateway overgrown with gray thornvines, examining a cracked tablet device on her forearm with gaunt focused face and natural human facial features, a well-defined human nose visible, patched brown traveling clothes stained with ash, a weathered satchel over her shoulder, dim overcast lighting, dark fantasy color palette of grays and muted ochres.
narrative: The device leads her to a half-buried settlement. Walls cracked. Gate shattered. But the signal is strong here — an old power node beneath the foundations. Mira enters through the ruin, scanning for survivors.
narration: The settlement exists. Barely. The walls are fractured stone, half-swallowed by gray thornvines that feed on magical residue. Mira stands at the broken gate. The device reads the infrastructure beneath — power conduits, old-world wiring, a node still drawing current from deep underground. The signal is clean. Stronger than anything she has found in months. She steps through the gate. The air changes. Warmer here. Someone has been burning fires. She hears movement inside. Not monsters. People. Her hand tightens on the bracer. Survivors mean complications. Survivors mean she cannot simply take what she needs and leave.

title: The Village Chief
prompt: Over-the-shoulder shot from behind Kael's broad armored shoulder, Mira visible beyond him in the settlement courtyard, Kael a gray-bearded scarred man in heavy leather armor with weathered hands gripping a crude spear, Mira standing small against a backdrop of damaged stone buildings with boarded windows, tense standoff composition, cold blue-gray lighting, dark fantasy atmosphere.
narrative: Kael meets her at the inner wall. He is the chief of what remains — forty-three people, mostly elderly and children. He does not trust outsiders. His spear stays level with her chest. He demands to know why she has come.
narration: Kael does not lower the spear. Gray beard, scar running from his left eye to his jaw. Leather armor patched with iron plates. His people hide behind him — forty-three souls, most of them too old or too young to fight. He has lost seven to the Hollow Things this season alone. He does not know this woman. He does not trust the glowing device on her arm. Magic burned the world. Magic brought the cataclysm. And now this stranger carries a piece of the old world's wreckage strapped to her skin like it belongs there. He keeps the spear steady.

title: Old Code, New Fire
prompt: Wide shot, Mira kneeling on the ground in the settlement's central square connecting her forearm device to a cracked stone conduit with glowing wires, Kael standing behind her arms crossed watching suspiciously, several villagers gathered at a distance, warm amber light emerging from the ground beneath Mira's hands, dark fantasy setting with ruined stone buildings, cinematic lighting.
narrative: Mira shows Kael what the device can do. She interfaces with the buried power node and restores a single light — a pale amber glow from a dead streetlamp. The villagers gasp. Kael is silent. The light holds.
narration: Mira presses her palm to the cracked conduit. The device does the rest — old algorithms searching old wiring, tracing power routes that survived the collapse. A streetlamp flickers. Dies. Flickers again. Then it holds. Pale amber light spills across the courtyard. The children stare. An old woman presses her hand to her mouth. No one has seen electric light in years. Kael says nothing. His jaw works beneath the beard. The spear lowers one inch. Mira does not celebrate. One light means nothing if the Hollow Things come. She needs to map the entire grid. She needs time she probably does not have.

title: What the Device Remembers
prompt: Close-up, Mira's face lit by pale blue holographic data projected from the bracer on her forearm, tears forming in her eyes, a well-defined human nose and natural human facial features visible, dark circles under her eyes, ash-smudged skin, the holographic display showing fragmented maps and threat markers, blue light casting sharp shadows, emotional dark fantasy atmosphere.
narrative: The AI device maps the local network and finds something worse — data from the cataclysm itself. Hollow Things are not random. They cluster around dead power nodes, feeding on residual energy. This settlement sits on one. They will come again. Soon.
narration: The data scrolls across her vision. Maps. Threat signatures. Decay patterns from the collapse itself, preserved in the device's memory like a scar that never healed. The Hollow Things are not random predators. They cluster where the old power died — feeding on residual energy the way maggots feed on rot. This settlement sits directly on a dead node. Mira stares at the numbers. The convergence window. Forty-eight hours. Maybe less. She closes her eyes. Opens them. The data does not change. She wipes her face with one hand. Her fingers come away wet. She did not know she was crying. It does not matter. She has work to do.

title: Forty-Three Against the Dark
prompt: Low angle shot looking up at Kael standing on a stone wall scanning the darkened horizon, his gray beard and scarred face silhouetted against a bruised sky, crude weapons — spears, axes, sharpened farm tools — leaning against the wall beside him, Mira below in the foreground wiring conduit into a defensive perimeter, tense dramatic lighting with deep shadows.
narrative: Mira tells Kael the truth. The Hollow Things are coming. Kael does not panic. He has buried too many for panic. He asks what she needs. She says she can build a defensive grid — but she needs every hand. Kael rallies his people.
narration: Kael listens. His face does not change. Forty-three people. Eleven can fight. The rest are old, young, or broken in ways that do not show. He has defended this place with spears and fire for two years. Seven people lost this season alone. He knows what is coming even before she finishes speaking. He asks one question. What does she need. Mira lists the requirements. Copper wire scavenged from the ruins. Stone conduits cleared of debris. Every working power cell dug from the cellars. Kael turns to his people. He does not give speeches. He tells them to work. They pick up tools. No one argues. Fear is a better motivator than hope.

title: Wiring the Bones
prompt: Medium close-up, Mira and Kael working side by side in a narrow stone trench, Mira connecting glowing wires from her bracer device into a conduit while Kael holds a heavy stone slab in place, their faces close together, a well-defined human nose and natural human facial features on Mira, Kael's scarred jaw tense with effort, warm amber light from the wiring contrasting cold blue shadows, gritty dark fantasy atmosphere.
narrative: Through the night, Mira and Kael work together. He clears debris. She rewires the grid. The old power node beneath the settlement begins to pulse again — weak, but alive. Kael watches the light spread across the walls and says nothing. The silence between them shifts.
narration: They work through the night. Kael does not ask how the device works. He does not ask about the old world. He clears rubble. Holds stone. Passes tools. Mira traces circuits that have been dead for a decade, coaxing power through corroded copper like threading a needle through scarred tissue. The node beneath them pulses. Weak. Irregular. But alive. Light creeps along the settlement walls in thin amber lines. Kael watches it spread. His hands are raw. He flexes his fingers and does not complain. Something has shifted between them. Not trust — not yet. Something smaller. Respect, maybe. Or exhaustion mistaken for kinship. Either way, the work continues.

title: They Come
prompt: High angle shot looking down at the settlement from above, its walls traced with glowing amber defense lines, dark shapes — Hollow Things, twisted shadowy creatures — massing at the perimeter in the surrounding ashfield, Mira standing at the central control point with her bracer raised, Kael below with his spear and fighters positioned at the walls, dramatic tense lighting with amber glow versus encroaching darkness.
narrative: The Hollow Things arrive at dawn. Twisted shapes moving through the ash — not quite animal, not quite human. The defensive grid holds at the first perimeter. Mira channels power through the bracer, lighting the wall-lines. The creatures recoil. But they do not retreat.
narration: Dawn brings them. Shapes in the ash. Not running — flowing, like smoke given hunger. Hollow Things. Their bodies are wrong — limbs too long, faces where faces should not be, mouths open in silence. The perimeter grid fires. Amber light flares along the wall. The first wave recoils. Burns. Falls back. Kael's fighters hold the line. Spears thrust through gaps in the stone. The sounds are wet and brief. Mira channels power through the bracer. Her arm aches. The device is not meant for this — sustained output drains it fast. The battery numbers drop. She watches them fall. The Hollow Things regroup. They learn fast. They always learn fast.

title: The Price of Light
prompt: Dutch angle shot, Mira collapsed against the central conduit with her bracer sparking and cracked, Kael standing over her with his spear raised toward approaching shadows, the amber grid flickering and dying in sections, one arm of Mira's bracer shattered with exposed wiring, villagers huddled behind them, chaotic tense lighting with intermittent amber flashes against deep darkness.
narrative: The grid fails at the south wall. Hollow Things breach. Kael's fighters fall back. Mira reroutes the remaining power — collapsing the outer grid to strengthen the inner wall. The bracer cracks further. The AI device whispers damage reports she ignores. The inner wall holds. Barely.
narration: The south wall goes dark. Three Hollow Things breach the gap before Kael's fighters can close it. A man screams. A spear snaps. Mira reroutes. The outer grid dies — every amber line winks out at once, plunging the perimeter into darkness. The remaining power floods the inner wall. Light blazes. The creatures that made it inside burn from within, shrieking soundlessly. The bracer cracks. A spiderweb of fractures spreads across its surface. The device whispers damage warnings in a voice she has not heard in years. She ignores it. The inner wall holds. Her arm goes numb. She cannot feel her fingers. Kael pulls her to her feet. His hands are sticky with blood. Not his.

title: What Remains
prompt: Wide shot, dawn light breaking over the settlement, the walls scarred and cracked but standing, amber lines still glowing faintly along the inner perimeter, Mira sitting on the ground with her broken bracer in her lap, Kael beside her holding a cup of water, villagers emerging from shelter into pale golden light, bodies of Hollow Things dissolving into ash in the foreground, hopeful dark fantasy atmosphere with warm dawn tones breaking through gray.
narrative: Morning comes. The Hollow Things dissolve into ash. The inner wall holds. Mira's bracer is destroyed — the AI device silent and dark. But the power node beneath the settlement still pulses. The grid can be rebuilt. Kael sits beside her. He does not speak for a long time. Then he asks what she needs.
narration: Dawn breaks slow and pale across the Ashfield. The Hollow Things dissolve where they fell — ash returning to ash, as if they were never here. Mira sits on the cracked stone with the dead bracer in her lap. The device is finished. Dark screen, silent circuits, cracked beyond repair. She stares at it anyway. Her arm still tingles where the interface burned. Kael sits beside her. He holds out a cup of water. She takes it. They sit in silence while the village wakes. The power node pulses beneath them. Weak but alive. Kael looks at the glowing lines along the inner wall. He asks what she needs to rebuild. Not the device. The grid. The defenses. The light. Mira drinks the water. It tastes like iron and ash. She tells him she needs copper wire. And time. And forty-three people who refuse to die.
